description: This figure consists of two subplots illustrating the effect of alloying elements on the κ-phase solvus in Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight ferrous alloys. Subplot (a) presents phase stability as a function of Al content (5 to 11 wt.%) in Fe-30Mn-xAl-1C alloys, with temperature (500 to 900 °C) as the y-axis. Three phase regions are marked: single γ (austenite) at high temperatures, γ + κ* (intergranular κ-carbide) at intermediate temperatures, and γ + κ + κ* (both intragranular and intergranular κ-carbide) at low temperatures. Solvus temperatures for both κ phases increase with rising Al content. Subplot (b) shows phase stability as a function of Mn content (23 to 30 wt.%) in Fe-xMn-7Al-1C alloys, with the same three phase regions; here, solvus temperatures decrease with rising Mn content. The figure confirms that Al elevates κ-carbide solvus while Mn reduces it, supporting optimized aging process design to tailor beneficial intragranular κ-carbide precipitation for improved mechanical performance of these lightweight steels.
